verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix/inner-membrane-facing protein — the catalytic F1 head of complex V. However, multiple independent surface-proteomics and cell-biology studies have documented that F1-ATP synthase subunits (including the beta subunit) traffic to the plasma membrane outer leaflet in a variety of contexts: (1) tumor/cancer cells display ecto-ATP synthase complexes on the outer PM face, where they function as angiostatin receptors and are therapeutic targets; (2) endothelial and certain immune cells display surface ATP synthase under activation or stress; (3) hepatocyte and adipocyte surface ATP synthase has been documented. This ecto-pool lacks a TM domain and appears context-dependent (cancer, activation, stress), qualifying as cell_state_induced. There is no own TM anchor; the surface display is restricted to specific cell states. No CD designation, but well-documented cancer/endothelial ecto-ATP synthase literature exists.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface display is well-documented in cancer/endothelial cells but the anchoring mechanism of the ecto-F1 complex to the outer PM leaflet (no TM domain) remains incompletely characterized.
